title: Mortality rate, under-5 (per 1,000 live births)
definition: Under-five mortality rate is the probability per 1,000 that a newborn baby will die before reaching age five, if subject to age-specific mortality rates of the specified year.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata

editorial.shortHook: A stark map of child survival.
editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Nigeria, Niger, Somalia, Fed. Rep., while the lightest include Estonia, Luxembourg, Belarus; that contrast is the fastest way to read the under-5 mortality pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Under-5 mortality highlights where basic health systems and living conditions still shape childhood survival.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Infant mortality can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Life expectancy can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Female adult mortality can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
regionalSignals: Health maps often separate income, public-health capacity, and conflict exposure.
Check both high-income and low-income anchors.

reviewNotes: Strong child-survival map and clearer than infant mortality for Daily.
Infant mortality is acceptable only as a hard-tier close distractor.
